original_abstract: The remarkable success of Transformers in natural language processing and computer vision has sparked significant interest within the time series community. Their inherent ability to capture long-range dependencies and complex interactions is particularly well-suited for temporal modeling, leading to substantial advancements across various applications. This paper provides a systematic review of Transformer-based architectures for time series, critically evaluating both their strengths and inherent limitations. We examine the evolution of these models through two primary lenses: first, from a structural perspective, we summarize the specific architectural adaptations developed to address the unique challenges of time series data; second, from an application perspective, we categorize existing research into core tasks such as forecasting, anomaly detection, and classification. To move beyond theoretical review, we conduct extensive empirical studies—including robustness testing, model complexity analysis, and seasonal-trend decomposition—to assess how Transformers behave in practice. We conclude by identifying open challenges and suggesting future research directions to guide further innovation in the field.
humanized_abstract: This paper aims to give a comprehensive survey about the recent developments of using Transformer models for time series forecasting from the perspective of their pros and cons. We present the development of Transformer-based models from two aspects. From the structural perspective, we look at the design choices and architectures that are introduced in these models in order to deal with characteristics of time series data. From the application perspective, we cluster recent works in four main categories of time series forecasting including forecasting, anomaly detection, classification and other applications. To back up our survey, we conduct thorough experimental studies on several benchmark datasets including robustness test, model complexity evaluation and seasonal-trend decomposition to examine the behavior of Transformer-based models. Finally, the survey is ended by discussing open challenges in the related area and providing suggestions for future works.
